results.title: Remarkable efficacy of temozolomide for relapsed spinal myxopapillary ependymoma with multiple recurrence and cerebrospinal dissemination: a case report and literature review.
results.abstract: Myxopapillary ependymomas are intradural tumors which grow from the terminal filum of the spinal cord. Although they are classified as WHO grade I, they sometimes cause cerebrospinal fluid dissemination or local recurrence. In this report, we describe a case in that temozolomide (TMZ) showed remarkable efficacy on a recurrent spinal myxopapillary ependymoma.
results.journal: European spine journal : official publication of the European Spine Society, the European Spinal Deformity Society, and the European Section of the Cervical Spine Research Society

results.title: [Atypical pseudotumoral neuromeningeal cryptococcosis in an immunocompromised patient treated for pontine glioma. Case report and literature review].
results.abstract: We report an atypical feature of neuromeningeal cryptococcosis presenting as spinal cystic arachnoiditis and cerebellar cryptococcoma in a child treated for pontine glioma.
